name: Lemon Balm
summary: Lemon Balm (Melissa officinalis) is a herb monograph focused on the leaf; aerial parts used in herbal preparations. It is associated with Europe, Mediterranean. GABAergic (GABA transaminase inhibition); mild cholinergic activity. Generally safe; mild sedation possible.
description: Lemon Balm is represented here as a monograph-style entry centered on the leaf; aerial parts. Key reported compounds include rosmarinic acid; citral; citronellal; linalool; flavonoids. Typical preparation forms: Leaf extracts, teas, tinctures. GABAergic (GABA transaminase inhibition); mild cholinergic activity. Interaction profile: Sedatives, thyroid medications. Use caution or avoid in: Hypothyroidism (caution).
mechanism: GABAergic (GABA transaminase inhibition); mild cholinergic activity
